---
title: "Join Us"
---

::: {.content-section}

We are always interested in hearing from motivated students and researchers who are passionate about motor learning and skill acquisition.

## Opportunities

<div class="research-cards" role="list">

<div class="research-card" role="listitem">
<div class="research-card-icon" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i></div>
### Doctoral Students
Looking to pursue a PhD in motor learning, skill acquisition, or related fields.
</div>

<div class="research-card" role="listitem">
<div class="research-card-icon" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-journal-bookmark"></i></div>
### Master's Students
Interested in thesis projects involving eye-tracking, perceptual-motor learning, or experimental motor learning research.
</div>

<div class="research-card" role="listitem">
<div class="research-card-icon" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-pencil-square"></i></div>
### Bachelor's Students
Seeking hands-on research experience in our lab.
</div>

<div class="research-card" role="listitem">
<div class="research-card-icon" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-graph-up-arrow"></i></div>
### Postdoctoral Researchers
With expertise in motor learning, cognitive neuroscience, or related areas.
</div>

<div class="research-card" role="listitem">
<div class="research-card-icon" aria-hidden="true"><i class="bi bi-globe"></i></div>
### Visiting Researchers
Interested in collaborative projects or research stays.
</div>

</div>

<div class="join-info-grid">

<div>

## What We Look For

Ideal candidates have:

- Strong interest in motor learning and skill acquisition
- Background in sport science, kinesiology, psychology, or related disciplines
- Enthusiasm for experimental research and data analysis
- Good communication skills in English
- (For graduate positions) Research experience and/or relevant academic background

</div>

<div>

## Research Areas

Our lab focuses on:

- Eye-tracking and gaze behavior in complex motor tasks
- Perceptual-motor learning in racing simulation
- Skill acquisition in sport and performance contexts
- Ecological psychology approaches to motor learning
- Human information processing in dynamic environments

</div>

</div>

---

::: {.join-box}

## How to Apply

If you are interested in joining, please contact Gal Ziv at [gal.ziv@lsu.lt](mailto:gal.ziv@lsu.lt)

Please include:

- A brief description of your research interests
- Your CV
- Any relevant academic transcripts (for student positions)

:::

:::
